Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Case To Trial
Off Calendar May-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bernhard and B. Douglas Robbins for Jennifer Kwok (petitioner). Phil Foster for Jeannie Kwong (respondent). Edward Zusman amd Elliot Rector for Gary Wong (respondent). Richard Bryan for Feng Ouyang (respondent).

Mr. Robbins presented his oral argument in opposition to the Court's tentative ruling. Mr. Foster argued why this matter should be dismissed. Mr. Zussman spoke to the issue of the reply. Mr. Bernhard discussed his efforts in contacting the various departments of 202, 204 and 206. The Court and counsel had a discussion regarding their efforts to set a trial date in this matter.

Having heard oral argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Respondent's Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Bring Case to Trial is granted under CCP 583.360. The statutory time limit in which to commence trial on this petition was extended by written stipulation to no later than June 26, 2017, and potentially by oral stipulation on the record to September 27, 2017. The plain meaning of the last stipulation between the parties continuing the trial date does not expressly waive Respondent's right to bring a motion to dismiss, nor does it extend the statutory time limit indefinitely. Petitioner has not met the burden to show by clear and convincing evidence that the statutory time limit was further tolled due to impossibility, futility, or impracticality. Petitioner has not shown that she acted with due diligence to bring this case to trial at all stages of the proceeding; Petitioner's papers show that she was aware that the extended statutory time limit in which to commence trial had run, and yet did not seek to specially set the petition for trial until after this Motion to Dismiss was filed. There is insufficient showing that Respondent is equitably estopped from bringing this Motion to Dismiss. Mr. Foster to prepare a proposed form of order that conforms to the tentative ruling and is approved as to form.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk:N. Wong, Bailiff: V. Jung. Court Reporter: Wendy Graves: CSR:6138.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bernhard for and with Jennifer Kwok (petitioner). Phil Foster for and with Jeannie Kwong (respondent). Edward Zusman for Gary Wong (respondent).

The Court stated that this case has a long and convoluted history and asked where we are? Mr. Foster reported that there is a trial set on February 27, 2017 and that the parties will be meeting with Judge Wiss after this hearing for further settlement discussions. The Court stated that from the pleadings it can not follow the money and requested charts or graphs.

Mr. Foster provided case background, explained that there are three trusts before the Court, explained that there is a tax exemption of three and a half million dollars and that the balance of the exemption has gone toward the gift to Jennifer's children. Mr. Bernhard presented his oral argument in support of his position as to why Jennifer should have received one million from the insurance policy. The Court and counsel have a brief discussion on when the insurance paid out the policy.

The Court stated that parties are to submit not for filing exhibits or charts that outline the following scenarios: 1) Where the money would have gone if the insurance policy beneficiary was changed and how many trusts funded. 2) Where did the money go when the beneficiary on the insurance policy was not changed. 3) Where has the money gone with the disclaimer. Parties are to submit simultaneously by February 3, 2017. The Court also informed the parties to submit a short memorandum regarding the lose of use claim. The Court continued the motion for summary judgment and respondent Gary Wong's motion for summary judgment to February 9, 2017 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk: Nancy Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Malohney, Not Reported, D501.

Off Calendar Jun-17-2016 Continued To Nov-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phil Foster and David Wakukawa (by CourtCall) for Jeanne Kwong; Carl Lipperberger for Lau Kwong, Larry Kwong, Amy Kwong and Sarah Kwong; Daniel Bernhard for Jennifer Kwok; Richard Bryan for Feng Ouyang.

The Court noted that he had received and reviewed counsel's joint statement. Mr. Bernhard stated that there is a satisfaction of judgment. He opined that the appeal of the disclaimer trial would affect a variety of issues, but not the life insurance dispute, and he suggested that the parties proceed to trial on that dispute. Mr. Foster stated that payment of the judgment is the biggest issue, with post-judgment interest accruing at the rate of $500,000 per year. He contended that the insurance dispute was related to the other matters subject to the appeal, and could not be resolved independently. He urged mediation and settlement of all issues in a global settlement, asserting that a global settlement would allow structuring of the deal in a way to minimize tax consequences. Mr. Lippenberger said that the parties have mediated for over four days, resulting in the parties getting farther apart rather than closer to a settlement. He asserted that if there were to be any mediation, it should be focused on the life insurance dispute. He agreed that most of the remaining issues were governed by the disclaimer appeal. Mr. Wakukawa stated that he was not repared to agree to mediation. He added that not much discovery has been conducted on the insurance dispute, so that matter is not ready for trial. The Court stated that he would be ordering the parties to appear before Judge Wiss in Department 305 on August 15, 2016 for a mandatory settlement conference. He set a status hearing in Department 204 for November 18, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 206. He ordered the matter to trial in Department 206 on February 27, 2017 at 9:30 am.
